sanskrit:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः।
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत्॥
transliteration:
yadi mām apratīkāram aśastraṃ śastra-pāṇayaḥ
dhārtarāṣṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣemataraṃ bhavet
literal_meaning: If, unresisting and weaponless, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra were to slay me on the battlefield, that would be better for me.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices a suicidal surrender born of moral collapse: preferring annihilation to the unbearable responsibility of harming loved ones, he externalizes death as mercy and re-labels passivity as virtue, revealing how acute ethical conflict can invert the survival instinct into a wish for self-erasure.
psychological_state: A high-functioning caregiver or leader who would rather be annihilated than act in a way that betrays their idealized self-image.
trigger_scenario: A doctor ordered to ration ventilators during a pandemic who silently hopes to catch the virus and die so they never have to choose who lives or dies.
anchor_text: Choosing inaction to avoid harm can itself become a deeper harm.
